next_steps: Send cerebrospinal fluid for Angiostrongylus PCR or antibody testing
Send serum and cerebrospinal fluid for parasitic serologies (e.g., Angiostrongylus, Gnathostoma, Strongyloides)
Perform magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the brain and spine with and without gadolinium contrast
Consult infectious disease and neurology specialists for further guidance
Continue supportive care and manage intracranial pressure if necessary
Obtain travel and dietary exposure history specifically for raw or undercooked mollusk/slug ingestion
Perform contrast-enhanced MRI of the brain and entire spine
Test cerebrospinal fluid for Angiostrongylus cantonensis DNA via PCR
Send serum and cerebrospinal fluid for specific parasitic serology (e.g., Angiostrongylus, Gnathostoma)
Consult infectious disease and neurology for specialized management including consideration of antiparasitic therapy and corticosteroids
Monitor intracranial pressure and manage appropriately
Obtain targeted history regarding ingestion of raw snails, slugs, or unwashed produce in endemic regions
